rice.task: Prepare nursery bed, treat seeds with bio-pesticides, sow seeds, and manage water level to 2 cm.
rice.criticalAlert: Ensure land is leveled perfectly to avoid water logging in patches.

rice.task: Puddle the main field twice, apply basal fertilizer (MOP, SSP, Urea), and transplant 25-day-old seedlings (2-3 per hill).
rice.criticalAlert: Maintain a standing water level of 2-3 cm for the first week post-transplanting.

rice.task: Perform first weeding using a cono-weeder, apply second split of Nitrogen (Urea), and monitor for Stem Borer.
rice.criticalAlert: Keep water level low (1-2 cm) during active tillering to encourage dense root-crown development.

rice.task: Apply final Nitrogen top-dress, keep constant water level (5 cm), and monitor for Blast disease and Brown Planthopper.
rice.criticalAlert: Do not let the soil dry out during flowering; moisture stress here reduces yield by 40%.

rice.task: Monitor grain milk stage. Guard against lodging from high winds. Keep soil moist but not flooded.
rice.criticalAlert: Drain all standing water 10-15 days before the expected harvest date to harden the soil.

rice.task: Harvest when 85-90% of panicles turn golden yellow. Dry grains on a clean canvas to 12% moisture level.
rice.criticalAlert: High moisture during storage leads to grain yellowing, seed rot, and severe market price cuts.

wheat.task: Till the field to a fine tilth. Treat seeds with Carboxin. Sow in rows spaced 22 cm apart at a depth of 5 cm.
wheat.criticalAlert: Sowing depth exceeding 6 cm severely delays seedling emergence and weakens the crop.

wheat.task: Apply first irrigation (critical CRI stage - 21 days post sowing). Apply the first top-dress of Nitrogen (Urea).
wheat.criticalAlert: Missing irrigation at CRI stage reduces potential tillering spikes by up to 30%.

wheat.task: Perform manual weeding or apply post-emergence herbicide. Apply second split of Nitrogen. Monitor for Yellow Rust.
wheat.criticalAlert: Keep alert for yellow stripes on leaves during foggy mornings; spray propiconazole immediately if spotted.

wheat.task: Provide third irrigation. Flowering begins. Guard against sudden high-temperature stress by maintaining soil moisture.
wheat.criticalAlert: Dry soil coupled with hot dry winds during anthesis leads to pollen sterility and empty spikes.

wheat.task: Provide final light irrigation. Grains are soft and milky. Keep field aerated.
wheat.criticalAlert: Avoid flood irrigation during high-speed winds in March to prevent crop lodging (falling over).

wheat.task: Harvest using a reaper or combine when leaves turn dry and straw becomes brittle. Sun-dry grains to 11% moisture.
wheat.criticalAlert: Store only in clean, shade-dried gunny bags. Use neem leaves or dry ginger to deter storage weevils.

cotton.task: Incorporate compost. Dibble seeds at 90 x 60 cm spacing. Apply pre-emergence herbicide to control early weeds.
cotton.criticalAlert: Maintain a firm, moist seedbed to facilitate quick seed germination.

cotton.task: Earthing up soil around plants. Apply first split of Nitrogen & Potassium. Set up pheromone traps for Bollworms.
cotton.criticalAlert: Monitor square-formation closely. Square shedding indicates nitrogen deficiency or early pest attack.

cotton.task: Perform second weeding. Spray micro-nutrients (Boron and Magnesium). Maintain uniform soil moisture.
cotton.criticalAlert: Water-logging during flowering causes massive flower and young boll drop. Clear all drainage channels.

cotton.task: Monitor for Pink Bollworm. Apply final split of Nitrogen. Spray neem-based insecticides if sucking pest counts cross ETL.
cotton.criticalAlert: Sucking pests like Jassids and Whiteflies cause leaf curling and transmit viral leaf roll disease.

cotton.task: Bolls start splitting open. Pick clean cotton from well-opened bolls in dry morning hours. Avoid picking damp bolls.
cotton.criticalAlert: Do not mix stained, infested, or dry-leaf-mixed cotton with clean picking; it drops the cotton grade.

cotton.task: Complete 2nd and 3rd pickings. Terminate the crop by grazing cattle or using stalk shredders to break the pest cycle.
cotton.criticalAlert: Do not keep the cotton crop standing into the winter. It encourages Pink Bollworm to carry over to next season.

default.task: Deep plow the field, incorporate organic manures, and submit soil samples to the local lab for testing.
default.criticalAlert: Allow the plowed soil to bake in the hot sun for 10-15 days to eradicate soil-borne pathogens.

default.task: Sow healthy certified seeds or transplant vigorous nursery saplings at proper recommended depths and spacing.
default.criticalAlert: Treat seeds with fungicides or bio-agents to protect against damping-off diseases.

default.task: Apply basal fertilizers based on soil twin values. Conduct manual weeding or apply selective herbicides.
default.criticalAlert: Always apply nitrogenous fertilizers when soil is moist to prevent nitrogen volatilization.

default.task: Irrigate at critical stages (e.g., flowering/fruiting). Monitor pest traps. Apply pest-suppression sprays if needed.
default.criticalAlert: Moisture stress at flowering irreversibly reduces seed/grain count and overall crop productivity.

default.task: Gradually reduce watering. Monitor crop maturity indicators (color change, hardness). Set up clean drying yards.
default.criticalAlert: Excessive irrigation during ripening stages delays maturity and reduces quality of final produce.

default.task: Harvest during dry weather. Dry produce to safe moisture storage levels. Bag in clean, sterilized storage units.
default.criticalAlert: Storing grains/produce at high relative humidity causes aflatoxin contamination and grain heating.
